gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: TWAP window shortening via cardinality-1 pool enables sandwich attack on swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn
candidate.claimed_root_cause: getTorusQuoteForTitanX caps secondsAgo to oldestObservation; with observationCardinality=1 this can be seconds, making the TWAP trivially manipulable
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker sandwiches swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn, receiving excess TORUS relative to TITAN_X input at protocol expense

live_preconditions.name: Contract holds TITAN_X for burn intervals
live_preconditions.observed_value: totalTitanXBurn=390047518149437255354831991687 (cumulative); live TITAN_X balance not directly shown

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Sets secondsAgo=900 then unconditionally caps it to oldestObservation if smaller; with cardinality=1 this can be seconds, producing a manipulable TWAP
amountOutMinimum is derived from the potentially-manipulated TWAP quote with only 10% tolerance; if TWAP is depressed 50%, minimum becomes 45% of fair value

attacker_path.actor: Any unprivileged EOA holding TORUS tokens
attacker_inputs: Timing of own TORUS->TITAN_X swap to depress pool price
Front-run TITAN_X->TORUS buy to inflate spot price before victim swap
Call or observe swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn as the sandwiched transaction
call_sequence: 1. Attacker sells TORUS for TITAN_X, depressing TORUS spot and TWAP (effective only if cardinality=1 and window is short)
2. Wait 2-5 blocks for short TWAP to fully reflect depressed price
3. Front-run: attacker buys TORUS with TITAN_X, pushing spot price up
4. swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn executes: getTorusQuoteForTitanX returns low quote -> adjustedTorusAmount is low -> swap fills at inflated spot without reverting
5. Back-run: attacker sells TORUS at inflated price, capturing sandwich profit
state_before: Protocol TITAN_X balance > 0 for burn interval
state_after: Protocol receives fewer TORUS than fair value for its TITAN_X

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TITAN_X held by TorusBuyAndProcess contract (burn allocation)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Net TITAN_X profit from sandwich; protocol TITAN_X is exchanged for below-fair-value TORUS
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol burn mechanism receives fewer TORUS per TITAN_X than fair market rate; value leaks to sandwich attacker
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker contributes no TITAN_X to the protocol yet extracts value by manipulating the price at which the protocol's TITAN_X is swapped; the 10% slippage guard is applied to the already-manipulated TWAP, not to fair price

setup: Fork mainnet at block 25530134 (live_context captured_at block)
Read pool.slot0() to confirm observationCardinality field (index 3 in tuple)
If cardinality != 1, test must fail with REJECT note
Read observations(0) to get blockTimestamp of oldest observation
transaction_sequence: Attacker swaps TORUS->TITAN_X in pool to depress price
vm.roll(block.number + 3) to let short TWAP settle
Attacker front-runs: swaps TITAN_X->TORUS to inflate spot
Attacker back-runs: sells TORUS for TITAN_X
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If observationCardinality > 1 and oldest observation age >= 900 seconds, full 15-minute TWAP is used and manipulation cost is prohibitive; finding must be REJECTED

rejection_reason: Not rejected: code path is confirmed at L1124-1128 of TorusBuyAndProcess.sol, slippage live value is 10 (confirmed), attack logic is sound. Held at NEEDS_LOCAL_PROOF because the critical precondition — pool observationCardinality=1 — is absent from live_context.json and cannot be asserted without a fork probe. The hard gate live_state_supports_preconditions and not_external_dependency_only cannot both be set true without on-chain confirmation of pool cardinality.
